lineage: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
That, being redeemed and quickened by the blood of Christ, we ought to prefer nothing to Christ.

title: That, being redeemed and quickened by the blood of Christ, we ought to prefer nothing to Christ.

source.title: ANF05. Fathers of the Third Century: Hippolytus, Cyprian, Caius, Novatian, Appendix

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: That, being redeemed and quickened by the blood of Christ, we ought to prefer nothing to Christ.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: In the Gospel the Lord speaks, and says: “He that loveth father or mother more than me, is not worthy of me; and he that loveth son or daughter more than me, is not worthy of me; and he that taketh not his cross and followeth me, is not worthy of me.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: So also it is written in Deuteronomy: “They who say to their father and their mother, I have not known thee, and have not acknowledged their own children, these have kept Thy precepts, and have observed Thy covenant.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Moreover, the Apostle Paul says: “Who shall separate us from the love of Christ? shall tribulation, or distress, or persecution, or hunger, or nakedness, or peril, or sword?

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: As it is written, Because for Thy sake we are killed all the day long, we are counted as sheep for the slaughter.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Nay, in all these things we overcome on account of Him who hath loved us.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: And again: “Ye are not your own, for ye are bought with a great price.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Glorify and bear God in your body.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: And again: “Christ died for all, that both they which live may not henceforth live unto themselves, but unto Him which died for them, and rose again.”
